sourceMovie.title: American Pie
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: American Pie works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: 10 Things I Hate About You
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If American Pie clicked for you, 10 Things I Hate About You (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after American Pie?
faq.answer: Try 10 Things I Hate About You, The 40 Year Old Virgin, The Girl Next Door—each shares DNA with American Pie while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is American Pie?
faq.answer: American Pie (1999) is best described as Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is American Pie part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "American Pie Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
